regions.eastern-caribbean: Eastern Caribbean weather for March 30, 2026, shows persistent rain showers with temperatures near 74-81°F and east-northeast winds steady at 8-13 knots across San Juan, St. Thomas, St. Croix, St. Maarten, St. Kitts, Antigua, and Turks & Caicos. Expect frequent wet conditions today through the next three days, with rain chances above 75% daily and no marine wind warnings currently in effect. Mariners should secure deck gear and exercise caution in exposed anchorages, especially in San Juan Bay and Charlotte Amalie Harbor, where wet conditions may reduce visibility and increase slippery surfaces. Passengers advised to carry rain gear and plan outdoor activities accordingly; fishing captains should monitor localized squalls and avoid extended operations during heavy showers.
regions.western-caribbean: Western Caribbean weather for March 30, 2026 remains mostly favorable with light easterly winds 10-13 knots and temperatures near 80°F. Cozumel and neighboring ports including Costa Maya, Belize City, Roatan, Grand Cayman, Ocho Rios, and Falmouth will see partly cloudy skies today with minimal rain risk. Expect an increase in rain showers late tomorrow through the next two days, with localized brief showers and overcast conditions possible. Mariners should monitor sea state and visibility around coastal anchorages; fishing captains should plan for intermittent rain and adjust gear accordingly while maintaining good communication for vessel safety.
regions.bahamas: Bahamas region weather for March 30, 2026: Currently, Nassau reports clear skies, 71°F, with ENE winds at 9 knots and a low 15% chance of rain. Expect winds to increase through the next three days, reaching 22 knots by Day 3 with occasional drizzle and overcast conditions. Small Craft Advisories may be warranted in eastern approaches near Freeport and Bimini by midweek due to freshening easterly winds. Cruise and fishing captains should monitor wind shifts and prepare for reduced visibility in light rain or drizzle; anchorage conditions remain generally stable but caution is advised for smaller vessels in exposed areas. Passengers should plan outdoor activities early today before clouds increase and light showers arrive.
regions.southern-caribbean: Southern Caribbean weather for March 30, 2026: Current conditions in Aruba show 79°F with ENE winds at 12 knots, partly cloudy skies, and a low 13% chance of rain. Expect increasing cloud cover and modest rain chances over the next three days, with overcast skies and scattered showers developing by Day 3. Winds remain steady from the ENE near 13-14 knots, well below Small Craft Advisory thresholds. Mariners should maintain caution for localized rain showers near Aruba, Curacao, Bonaire, and Cartagena; anchorage conditions remain stable but visibility may reduce briefly during showers. Cruise and fishing operations can proceed with normal caution; carry rain gear and monitor updates for any sudden weather changes.
regions.central-caribbean: Central Caribbean conditions for March 30, 2026: Grand Cayman reports steady ENE winds 17-19 knots with partly cloudy skies and a 35-38% chance of scattered showers through Day 2. By Day 3, expect a shift to easterly winds near 14 knots with increased rain showers and a 43% rain probability. Roatan, Belize City, Cozumel, and Costa Maya will see similar trade wind flow and intermittent light rain typical of tropical moisture this time of year. Mariners should maintain vigilant watch for sudden squalls; small craft operators in exposed anchorages, particularly around Belize City and Roatan, should secure loose gear and monitor local conditions for brief gusts. Passengers advised to plan outdoor activities with some flexibility due to isolated showers and moderate winds.
regions.lesser-antilles: Lesser Antilles briefing for March 30, 2026: Barbados reports 77°F with ENE winds at 11 knots under partly cloudy skies and a low 13% rain chance. Through the next three days, expect steady ENE to E winds 12-14 knots with mostly overcast skies and isolated light showers, highest rain chance 23% on March 31. Mariners should note seas are moderate; no Small Craft Advisories or warnings are in effect. Fishing captains should plan for stable trade winds and minimal rain, while yacht operators in St. Lucia, Martinique, and Dominica can expect similar conditions—maintain routine anchorage checks as cloud cover may reduce daytime visibility. Passengers should prepare for mild weather with light wind and occasional clouds, ideal for outdoor activities but carry light rain gear for brief showers.
regions.los-angeles: Los Angeles region ports are seeing calm conditions this morning with light northwest winds at 1 knot, partly cloudy skies, and temperatures near 56 degrees. Expect light southerly winds around 5 knots and patchy fog near coastal areas today, especially around Los Angeles/Long Beach harbors; visibility may be reduced early, so exercise caution when maneuvering in and out of Marina del Rey and Catalina Island anchorages. Winds increase WSW to 12 knots with overcast skies Tuesday and Wednesday, with a 26 percent chance of rain both days—yacht and fishing captains should secure loose gear and monitor forecasts for potential worsening conditions. Cruise operators should advise passengers to plan for cooler, damp weather midweek and prepare for restricted outdoor activities due to cloud cover and light precipitation.
regions.ensenada: Ensenada region is clear and calm this morning with light southerly winds at 2 knots and 60°F. Expect a light westerly breeze increasing to 9 knots by afternoon with isolated drizzle, though rain chances remain minimal. Over the next three days, winds will shift west to west-southwest and pick up to 13 knots by day three, accompanied by increasing cloud cover and a higher chance of rain up to 32 percent. Mariners should secure lighter vessels in Ensenada harbor and Punta Banda anchorage ahead of the breezier, overcast conditions later in the week; fishing captains should plan for reduced visibility in fog on day two near Islas Todos Santos. Passengers should carry light rain gear and be prepared for cooler, damp mornings through midweek.
regions.cabo-san-lucas: Cabo San Lucas and surrounding ports will see mild temperatures near 75°F today with northwest winds increasing to 16 knots under overcast skies, but no rain expected. Conditions remain stable through the next three days with winds 12-19 knots from the northwest to north-northwest, alternating between partly cloudy and overcast. Mariners should note winds remain below Small Craft Advisory thresholds; however, fishing and yacht captains should secure lighter vessels and monitor wind shifts, especially in La Paz anchorage where afternoon gusts may increase. Cruise passengers can enjoy calm seas and pleasant weather for shore excursions with minimal weather disruption.
regions.mazatlan: Mazatlan region maritime conditions remain stable for March 30, 2026. Winds are northwest at 5 knots, increasing to west-northwest 7 to 12 knots over the next three days with mostly partly cloudy to overcast skies and minimal rain chances below 5 percent. Mariners operating near Mazatlan, Topolobampo, and Altata can expect calm seas and good visibility for navigation and fishing activities, with no advisories in effect. Cruise passengers should enjoy comfortable weather ashore; yacht and fishing captains should maintain standard watch routines with no expected wind or weather hazards.
regions.puerto-vallarta: Puerto Vallarta and surrounding ports can expect light and variable winds today, northeast at 3 knots under partly cloudy skies with no rain threat. Mariners should note a shift to northwest winds around 7 knots with overcast conditions tomorrow, then west-northwest winds 7-8 knots and partly cloudy skies through day three. Anchorage remains calm with minimal chop, ideal for fishing and yacht operations in Puerto Vallarta, Punta Mita, Yelapa, and Chacala. Passengers should enjoy stable conditions but carry light layers for cooler overcast periods tomorrow.
regions.western-mediterranean: Western Mediterranean briefing for March 30, 2026: Barcelona currently reports 51°F with WNW winds at 5 knots under partly cloudy skies and no rain. Winds will increase slightly to 8 knots WNW today with overcast conditions but remain below Small Craft Advisory thresholds. Expect similar light winds and mostly overcast skies in Valencia, Palma de Mallorca, Ibiza, Malaga, Cadiz, Lisbon, and Gibraltar. Fog development is likely by Day 3, especially near coastal anchorages such as Palma and Ibiza; mariners should prepare for reduced visibility and exercise caution during navigation. Passengers should plan outdoor activities for today as conditions are mild and dry, but carry layers for cooler mornings and evenings.
regions.central-mediterranean: Central Mediterranean briefing for March 30, 2026: Naples is currently 55°F with light northeast winds at 3 knots and partly cloudy skies, no rain expected today. However, a significant weather shift is underway with rain showers forecasted through April 1, accompanied by increasing northeast to north-northeast winds up to 14 knots. Mariners should anticipate wet decks and reduced visibility near Naples and Civitavecchia; small craft should monitor conditions as gusts approach advisory thresholds. Cruise passengers are advised to plan indoor activities for the next two days, while yacht and fishing captains should secure gear and prepare for choppy seas, especially off Sardinia and Corsica where winds will strengthen.
regions.eastern-mediterranean: Eastern Mediterranean briefing for March 30, 2026: Athens (Piraeus) begins clear and calm with light southwest winds at 2 knots and 59°F. Expect overcast skies by afternoon with south-southwest winds increasing to 7 knots and temperatures rising to 67°F; no rain is forecast through the day. Fog may develop overnight into March 31, reducing visibility near coastal ports including Santorini, Mykonos, and Rhodes, with a slight chance of light rain (8%). By April 1, rain is likely with northeast winds around 9 knots and temperatures dropping to near 59°F; vessels should prepare for wet conditions and reduced visibility, especially in port approaches at Izmir, Cyprus, and Haifa. Fishing captains should monitor fog and rain for safe operations, while yacht and cruise vessels should confirm anchorage readiness in Rhodes and Corfu due to shifting winds and potential wet decks. No marine warnings are in effect at this time.
